topic.title: HTML Input Types
topic.description: The type attribute controls keyboard layout, validation, and the visual widget for each input.

content.text: HTML Input Types: type, autocomplete, and Native Validation

content.title: type='number' Is Not for All Numeric Data
content.text: type='number' is designed for quantities users can increment or compare, like price filters or age. It is wrong for data that happens to be numeric but is not a quantity: phone numbers, credit card numbers, PINs, and postal codes should use type='text' with inputmode='numeric' and pattern='[0-9]*'. type='number' strips leading zeros, shows a spinner UI, and allows 'e' for scientific notation, all of which break these use cases.

examples.title: Input Type Gallery: All Major Types in Context
examples.description: This example shows every major input type in a realistic form context: text family (text, email, password, search, url, tel), picker types (date, time, datetime-local, color), selection types (checkbox, radio), numeric types (number, range), and file input, each with label, autocomplete, and constraint attributes.
examples.code_snippet:
Input Type Gallery

Text family
Mobile: @ is prominent. Validates @ + domain format.
autocomplete="new-password" signals registration context.
Dial-pad keyboard. Use pattern= for format validation.
Validates scheme://host format.
Picker types
Value format: YYYY-MM-DD. Picker shows local format.
step="1800" = 30-minute intervals.
Value always #rrggbb hex.
Selection types
Unchecked: not submitted. Checked: name=value submitted.
Skill level (type="radio")
Numeric types
Spinner. Only for quantities — not PINs or postcodes.
5
Always display current value — range has no visible indicator.
File input
PDF or Word. accept= filters shown file types. Form needs enctype="multipart/form-data".
Action inputs reset clears all fields to their initial HTML values.
examples.output: Page renders six fieldsets. Text family shows five labelled inputs each with a hint line. Picker types shows date with min/max, time with step, and a colour picker. Selection shows a checkbox and an inline radio group. Numeric shows a number spinner and a range slider with live value. File shows an accept-filtered input. Actions shows submit and reset buttons.

examples.title: inputmode, pattern, autocomplete, and Mobile Optimisation
examples.description: This example demonstrates the inputmode attribute for overriding mobile keyboard without changing validation, the pattern attribute for regex validation with custom error messages, autocomplete token reference for identity and payment fields, and the type=number vs type=text with inputmode=numeric distinction for PINs and postcodes.
examples.code_snippet:
Input Attributes Reference

1. inputmode: keyboard without changing type

inputmodeMobile keyboardUse case
text Standard QWERTYDefault
numeric 0-9 onlyPIN, postcode, OTP
decimal 0-9 + decimal pointPrices, measurements
tel Dial pad 0-9 + * #Phone numbers
email @ and . prominentEmail on type=text
url / and . prominentURL on type=text
search Go/Search action keySearch fields
none No keyboard shownCustom input widgets
type="text" preserves leading zeros; inputmode="numeric" shows numpad.
Six-digit PIN code. type=text keeps numeric string intact.

2. pattern: custom regex validation

pattern="[a-zA-Z0-9_]{3,20}". title= sets the validation message.
type="tel" + pattern= = dial-pad + regex validation.

3. autocomplete token reference


autocomplete="name"              Full name
autocomplete="given-name"        First name
autocomplete="family-name"       Last name
autocomplete="email"             Email address
autocomplete="tel"               Phone number


autocomplete="username"          Login username
autocomplete="new-password"      New password (registration)
autocomplete="current-password"  Existing password (login)
autocomplete="one-time-code"     OTP / 2FA code (SMS autofill)


autocomplete="street-address"    Full street
autocomplete="address-level2"    City
autocomplete="postal-code"       Postcode / ZIP
autocomplete="country"           Country code


autocomplete="cc-number"         Card number
autocomplete="cc-name"           Name on card
autocomplete="cc-exp"            Expiry MM/YY
autocomplete="cc-csc"            CVV / CVC

4. type="number" correct vs wrong use cases

Wrong: type="number" for postcode — strips leading zeros

<input type="number" name="postcode">
"07001" becomes 7001. Shows spinner. Allows "1e5". Breaks postcodes.

Right: type="text" + inputmode="numeric" + pattern

<input type="text" name="postcode" inputmode="numeric" pattern="[0-9]{5}" maxlength="5">

Wrong: type="number" for credit card number

<input type="number" name="card_number">
Strips leading zeros. Allows e notation. Shows spinner. Breaks 16-digit cards.

Right: type="text" + inputmode="numeric" + autocomplete

<input type="text" name="card_number" inputmode="numeric" maxlength="19" autocomplete="cc-number" pattern="[0-9 ]{13,19}"> 
<!-- type="number" correct use cases: quantities only -->
<input type="number" name="quantity" min="1" max="99" step="1">
<input type="number" name="price_filter" min="0" step="0.01">
<input type="number" name="age" min="0" max="120">
examples.output: Page renders four sections. The inputmode table lists eight values with keyboard descriptions and use cases, followed by live PIN and postcode inputs. The pattern section shows two live inputs with regex validation turning red when invalid. The autocomplete section shows a dark code block with 17 tokens grouped by category. The correct vs wrong section shows two pairs of red and green cards then a dark code block with quantity examples.

subtopics.title: Text, Email and Password Inputs
subtopics.description: type=text is the default; email triggers @ keyboard and format validation; password masks input.

text.value: The three most common input types form a text-entry family.

examples.title: Text Family with Constraints, autocomplete, and Password Toggle
examples.description: This example shows all six text-family types with appropriate autocomplete tokens, constraint attributes, a password show/hide toggle that swaps type between password and text, and the difference between type=email single and multiple for comma-separated addresses.
examples.code_snippet:
Text, Email, and Password Inputs

Account details
minlength="2" maxlength="80"
Browser validates @ + domain format on submit.
multiple: accepts a comma-separated list of addresses.
At least 8 characters.
Web presence
Must start with https:// to pass validation.
Dial-pad on mobile. pattern= validates format.
Same as text; browser may add a clear (X) button.

Autocomplete tokens for text-family inputs

autocomplete="name"              Full name
autocomplete="given-name"        First name only
autocomplete="family-name"       Last name only
autocomplete="email"             Email address
autocomplete="tel"               Phone number
autocomplete="url"               Website URL
autocomplete="username"          Login username
autocomplete="new-password"      New password (registration)
autocomplete="current-password"  Existing password (login)
examples.output: Page renders two fieldsets and a code block. Account details shows full name (text), email with @ validation hint, CC email with multiple attribute, and password with a Show/Hide toggle that swaps the type between password and text and updates its aria-label. Web presence shows url with scheme validation, tel with pattern, and search with clear button note. The code block lists nine autocomplete tokens.

examples.title: Number, Date, File, Checkbox, and Radio with Constraints
examples.description: This example shows type=number for quantity with min/max/step, type=date with selectable range limits, type=time with 30-minute step intervals, type=file with accept and multiple, a checkbox group with fieldset, and a radio group with fieldset and legend, each with the correct constraint attributes and accessibility annotations.
examples.code_snippet:
Number, Date, File, Checkbox, Radio

Numeric inputs
min=1 max=8. Browser validates range on submit.
step="0.01" allows decimals. Remove step for any decimal.
Date and time
Stored: YYYY-MM-DD. Picker shows local date format.
step="1800" = 30-min intervals. Value: HH:MM.
No timezone stored. Value: YYYY-MM-DDTHH:MM.
File upload
PDF or image. Multi-select with Ctrl/Cmd. Requires enctype="multipart/form-data" on form.
Add-ons (select any)
Screen reader: "Add-ons, group. Meal, checkbox, not checked."
Seat class
Screen reader: "Seat class, group. Economy, radio button, checked, 1 of 3."
examples.output: Page renders six fieldsets. Numeric shows a number spinner for seats (1-8) and a decimal price input. Date/time shows date picker with 2026 range, time picker with 30-minute steps, and datetime-local. File shows a multi-file picker with PDF/image accept filter. Checkbox group shows three add-ons with a blue accessibility announcement card. Radio group shows three seat classes with its announcement card.

subtopics.title: Range and Color Inputs, Submit and Reset Buttons
subtopics.description: range is a slider; color opens a native colour picker; submit sends the form; reset clears fields.

text.value: These four types cover sliders, colour selection, and form actions.

and should be avoided in most forms because users click it accidentally. Reference:

content.title: type='reset' Is Rarely Appropriate
content.text: Users frequently click reset by accident when aiming for submit, losing all their form data. There is almost never a legitimate reason to clear all fields at once. If you need a cancel action, navigate away or clear fields individually with JavaScript. If you must include reset, position it far from submit and style it clearly differently.

examples.title: Range Slider, Color Picker, Submit, and Reset with Live Preview
examples.description: This example shows a range slider with live value display, a range slider using datalist for labelled tick marks, a color picker with live hex value and colour swatch, the input type=submit vs button type=submit comparison, the reset input with usage annotation, and the aria-valuetext pattern for accessible slider announcements.
examples.code_snippet:
Range, Color, Submit, and Reset

Range inputs
60%
Arrow keys also move the slider by step.
Medium
datalist adds tick marks and label snapping.
Color input
#2563eb
Value always #rrggbb. No alpha support.
Form action inputs 
<!-- input type=submit: text label only (value attribute) -->
<input type="submit" value="Save settings">

<!-- button type=submit: supports HTML content (icons, etc.) -->
<button type="submit">
  <svg .../> Save settings
</button>
<!-- Prefer button for icons, rich labels, or custom styling -->
Reset note: clicking "Clear form" resets every field on this page to its initial HTML value. Position reset far from submit. Avoid in most production forms.

Range accessibility: aria-valuetext

<input type="range" id="vol"
  min="0" max="100" step="10" value="60"
  aria-valuetext="60 percent"
  oninput="this.setAttribute('aria-valuetext',
    this.value + ' percent')">

<!-- Screen reader: "Volume, slider, 60 percent" -->
<!-- Without aria-valuetext: "Volume, slider, 60" -->
examples.output: Page renders three fieldsets and a code block. Range shows a volume slider where dragging updates the percentage live, and a priority slider with datalist tick marks that updates label text. Color shows a swatch and hex value updating live as the picker changes. Submit/Reset shows a code block comparing input vs button submit, then live submit and reset buttons with an amber warning. The accessibility section shows a dark code block with aria-valuetext pattern.

seo_metadata.meta_title: HTML Input Types | type, inputmode, autocomplete and Validation Guide
seo_metadata.meta_description: Master all HTML input types: text, email, password, number, date, file, checkbox, radio, range, color, submit and reset with inputmode, pattern, autocomplete, and constraint validation.
